description: Governance coverage ledger for every specification that steers Datum development. Enforced by scripts/check_spec_governance.py (wired into scripts/run_drift_gates.sh). Every .md under enforced_globs plus every tracked_doc and authority_doc MUST have an entry. Each entry needs only a 'class' — 'governed' (active spec), 'doctrine' (ratified decision record, controlling), 'pending' (known orphan awaiting disposition), 'historical' (superseded/frozen) — plus optional reason/notes fields. Behavioral enforcement lives in the proof gates and write-fence gates, not in per-doc ledger prose. See CLAUDE.md -> 'Specification Governance (controlling)'.

specs/PROGRAM_SPEC.md.notes: Reconciled 2026-07-06: legacy milestone master. M0–M4 exit criteria + v1 scope ('no GUI/create/edit/export') are HISTORICAL (original plan, not current scope); still-live governed content is the subordinate-spec ownership map + Standards Integration Gate. Not in the CLAUDE.md authority ordering; current sequencing is the PROGRESS Active Frontier + decisions.

specs/M7_FRONTEND_SPEC.md.reason: M7 review-spike frontend spec; superseded by GUI decisions 014/015 and the North Star. Not an active implementation target (its own header still self-declares Active; the governed truth is historical).

docs/decisions/PRODUCT_MECHANICS_013_GUI.reason: Tombstone. The GUI supervision-reflection track was a misfire, reverted in 7e352f4 (2026-06-23); record retained only to close the vacated 013 slot. Superseded by decisions 014/015 and docs/contracts/UI_LAYOUT_SYSTEM_CONTRACT.md.

enforcement.gap: all public datum.* prefixes are registry-owned; CLI clap and daemon dispatch remain target projections

enforcement.decision: genesis remains non-journaled; future visible genesis evidence must be a non-mutation sidecar

docs/decisions/PRODUCT_MECHANICS_OPEN_QU.remediation: Ratified resolutions log (e.g. IPC-7351B basis) should be consolidated into numbered decision records (010). Do not add new ratifications here.

docs/ALTIUM_LESSONS.md.notes: Competitive UX research — the professional-PCB UX benchmark (Properties Panel, keyboard routing, granular ECO, rule-query-as-command) feeding decision 019 / DATUM_GUI_PRODUCT_SPEC. Reference research; architectural lineage per CLAUDE.md.

docs/HORIZON_ANALYSIS.md.notes: Architectural-lineage study (Horizon EDA pool model + tool-lifecycle interaction + project-manager/editor cross-probe). Reference research per CLAUDE.md lineage; input to GUI interaction model.

docs/EAGLE_BLUEPRINT.md.notes: Architectural-lineage study (Eagle engine + interactive command-line paradigm, CCMDDrive). Reference research per CLAUDE.md lineage; input to GUI command/palette model.

docs/COMPETITIVE_ANALYSIS.md.notes: Competitive EDA landscape/positioning (KiCad/Altium/Horizon/OrCAD/PADS) — UX justification and what-to-avoid. Reference research.

docs/USER_WORKFLOWS.md.notes: End-to-end user workflow/task-flow research (analysis-first, write/modify, native authoring, AI-in-the-loop). Input to GUI product model. NOTE: currently CLI/MCP-centric; GUI workflows to be derived under decision 019.

docs/AUTHORING_TOOLS.md.notes: Authoring-action tool semantics (place/wire/move/route/delete) — the interaction grammar behind GUI edit tools.

docs/COMMERCIAL_INTEROP_STRATEGY.md.notes: Commercial interop roadmap (Altium/PADS/OrCAD migration paths). Reference (import frozen per decision 011); the only place OrCAD/PADS are discussed beyond passing mentions.

docs/INTEROP_SCOPE.md.notes: Interop scope reference (import/export role). Reference (import frozen per decision 011).

docs/RESEARCH_TRACEABILITY.md.notes: Research->spec traceability convention. Now itself tracked (was orphaned); should be wired toward enforcement as research governance matures.

docs/STANDARDS_AUDIT_BATCH_1_GUIDANCE.md.remediation: Standards Audit Batch 1 guidance; referenced by the PROGRESS batch section. Govern, or mark historical once the batch closes.

docs/STANDARDS_COMPLIANCE_INTEGRATION_GU.remediation: Research->spec integration meta-guidance; partially realized in STANDARDS_COMPLIANCE_SPEC. Fold into that spec or govern in the standards governance pass.

docs/CHECKING_ARCHITECTURE.md.reason: Non-normative design rationale; superseded by the governed specs/CHECKING_ARCHITECTURE_SPEC.md (gated via check_spec_parity standards_check_surface). The doc self-declares non-normative.

docs/DECOMPOSITION_PLAN.md.reason: Built around the size-budget gate system retired 2026-07-02; decomposition is now organic and governance-triggered, not gate-scheduled.

docs/DECOMPOSITION_BACKLOG.md.reason: Built around the size-budget gate system retired 2026-07-02; decomposition is now organic and governance-triggered, not gate-scheduled.

docs/PRODUCTION_CLEANUP_MANIFEST.md.remediation: Point-in-time worktree cleanup manifest (dated 2026-06-19); confirm whether still in use or retire as historical.

docs/LIBRARY_BOOTSTRAP_STRATEGY.md.remediation: Open library-bootstrap/content-strategy discussion; ratify into a numbered decision record (PRODUCT_MECHANICS_0xx) + PROGRESS row once the owner resolves D4 (offline vs optional-online) and the content-path A/B fork.

docs/gui/DATUM_GUI_MENU_BINDINGS.md.notes: Menu-command binding reference under decision 019; maps each GUI menu item to its verb/builder/CLI mechanism and records the write-path plumbing prerequisites. Derived from the 2026-07-05 four-source capability inventory.

docs/gui/DATUM_GUI_DESIGN_SPEC.md.notes: The GUI 'how' — ratified visual/interaction design under decision 019 on the systems of 014/015, in the pro-audio (Bitwig/Ableton) idiom. Controlling visual prototype: docs/gui/prototypes/board-editor.html. Owner-authored design; co-developed with the prototype.

docs/gui/DATUM_GUI_CONTEXT_MENU_CONTENT..notes: Per-object-type marking-menu content (PCB + schematic): cardinal-4 / secondary / overflow / sub-wheels with engine-op mapping. Realizes the Context Menu System content in DATUM_GUI_DESIGN_SPEC; research basis in research/gui-context-menus/CONTEXT_MENU_RESEARCH.md.

docs/gui/DATUM_GUI_PHASE_1_SPEC.md.notes: Precise sequenced Phase 1 GUI build spec under decision 019 (application shell + board render fidelity, READ-ONLY, no write path): deliverables D1-D7, reuse map, acceptance gates, and a binding 'Do NOT' list to keep Codex/execution on rails. Realizes the product spec's Phase 1 Recovery Slice.

docs/gui/DATUM_GUI_PARAMETRIC_TOOLING.md.notes: Deep/parametric tools: the parameter spaces of flagship ops (Align discipline, parametric array/pattern placement, thermal-via arrays, impedance-aware diff-pair routing) and the tri-modal contract (marking-menu presets / tool inspector / AI intent / CLI all drive one typed op). Lean 'capability = parameter of a small verb set' made concrete; the depth target the not_built verbs build toward.

docs/gui/menu_model.csv.notes: GUI menu-model authoring surface (spreadsheet / source of truth). Built to menu_model.json by scripts/menu_model_csv.py; CSV<->JSON sync enforced by 'menu_model_csv.py check' in run_drift_gates.sh, contents validated by check_menu_model.py. The human-input -> development + project-management instrument; verb-backed vs not_built counts are the live completeness metric.

docs/gui/menu_model.json.notes: Built artifact from menu_model.csv (do NOT hand-edit menubar/marking_menus; edit the CSV, run menu_model_csv.py build). Read by the GUI; gated by check_menu_model.py + the CSV<->JSON sync gate.

docs/gui/icon_set.json.notes: Declared GUI icon set (Tabler MIT base + custom EDA glyphs); every menu_model icon must be declared here; gated by check_menu_model.py. to_author entries = the glyph authoring worklist.

docs/gui/DATUM_GUI_WRITE_PATH_PLAN.md.notes: Bounded four-item implementation plan under decisions 019 + 017 for the GUI->engine journaled write path. Planning artifact; execution requires authorization. Status tracked in PROGRESS Scope Integration; W2 edits decision 017, W3 edits MCP_API_SPEC, W1 edits ENGINE_SPEC on execution.

docs/gui/REFERENCE_STUDY.md.notes: GUI design-research input under decision 019; competitor/reference UI study feeding DATUM_GUI_PRODUCT_SPEC (IA/interaction/visual language). Reference input, not standalone roadmap authority.
